item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't get at first how the Foundation could tell that 1207 isn't a mirror, but on rereading it I gather it's implied that the effect doesn't work if it's mediated through a camera or some other form of indirect observation.

Then I went reading through the comments and discovered that the Foundation originally couldn't tell, and thinking about the version of this article where the sign is staring you in the face while the Foundation swears up and down that it's a miror feels subtly creepy in a nice clever way, while the article as-is doesn't do much for me.

I gather from the comment history that it was this version or nothing. Oh well.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image is an adaptation of Luis Camnitzer's "This is a mirror. You are a written sentence. 1966." As such, this remix without the author's permission is a violation of their copyright and thus the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the new version better. I didn't like the recovery story or the conversation between Carrera and the site director that much, and the new version seems to get the same story across better. As for explaining the joke, eh, I figured or that this is a sign that is perceived as a mirror from the name "Not a Mirror" and the photo.

What I don't like is the mix of effects. Why the dyslexia? It makes sense when you consider the phrase, but not in combination with the other effects. Is that an unintended side effect? Also the new version is at a disadvantage since now you read three separate effects and it feels disjointed.

Oh hey, now I get it: It gets you thinking. I dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, people hated on AWCY a lot for a time.

Never got it, myself, they have always been one of my favorite GOIs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you had to spell out how it was spelled out and that's pretty funny

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first couple of times I read this I actually did not realize "mirror" was misspelled as "miror". Which is probably why I find this just a little bit more unsettling than I should or normally would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally like it both ways, though I think it would be interesting to have this one set up with iteration tabs (eg 2317), either with the current revision first or the original.

That said, this is one fucked up SCP, and that's coming from someone with life-long body image issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the part where someone threatens to slap you around a bit with a large trout if you don't turn your post into a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, that's why I left the original description in. The Foundation was fooled by this thing for a while. Then they started to notice some weird details about it (and I mean, weird even when we take into account that it's an SCP object). The first such weird detail was the fact that the "pink and red hearts" don't appear consistently to different people. (How many hearts? How are they arranged? Are they just along the top of the frame, or along the bottom, or all around it, or…?)

Even though the original version included the site director saying "no, permission denied, it's not interesting enough, just put it in storage"… well, the Foundation audits itself periodically, and checks its own inventory. And when inventory entries for a single item don't match, and you're the SCP Foundation, you look into it more closely. And then you check the photos of the object, and you e-mail copies of the photos to people at other sites just in case your own judgment or perception has been compromised by a cognitohazard. Standard practice.

And the people off-site say "these photos are not of a glass reflective surface held in a white wooden frame. You're manifesting strong indicators of cognitive disruption; we recommend a double-blind analysis. Also, 'MIRROR' has six letters, not five; normally we'd make a comment about your spellcheck being broken, but under the circumstances, this might be a further anomalous aspect."

And you read this e-mail, and you think to yourself… huh. You take out a pen and paper, and try to write the word "MIRROR". You count the letters as you write them, and realize that you can't write it with six letters. You can write "happy" with three consecutive 'p's, and "fussy" with three consecutive 's's, and "worry" with five consecutive 'r's, but you can't write "miror" with any additional 'r's.

You take out a fresh piece of paper, and switch the pen to your non-dominant hand. Then you write the words "SCP FOUNDATION, SECURE CONTAIN PROTECT", but starting at the end and working backwards — and not just by the order of letters, but with the order of gestures, too: for N, you'd normally start at the bottom, move up, and then diagonally down to the right, then up again, but now you start at the top, move down, and then diagonally up to the left, then down again.

It takes a few minutes to accomplish this, and it takes more than one try because you keep getting the letters backwards or in the wrong order or both, but you do manage it. And you're satisfied. Then you try the same thing with the word "MIRROR". Six letters. Six. Not five. Six. R, O, R, another R, I, M. And you manage it. Six letters. By forcing your brain to route the act of handwriting through three distinct inversion filters, you've managed to write the word "MIRROR" with six shaky, scribbly, smeary letters. You stare at the page, and start to tremble. It feels… it feels wrong, you feel terrified, and guilty, and so desperately sad, and furious, and your head hurts and your heart is pounding and suddenly your stomach does something horrible and you puke all over your desk and fall over backwards.

And that is when the investigation into SCP-1207 is re-opened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The question becomes, when is it narratively okay for the Foundation to do something stupid. They are human after all, and thus fully capable of mistakes. They are intelligent and capable enough for mistakes to be abnormal, but a Foundation incapable of mistakes is no more realistic than one that engages in wacky hijinks.

Not really advocating either way here, I'm just waxing philosophical to see what comes of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First version mighta been a work of art, sure, but the Foundation is emotionally dead. And they're not stupid. First version assumes the Foundation is kinda stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, at the risk of just repeating myself, I gotta say I agree with all of this. The first version was a work of art, man…

But hey, it's at +60 now, so the plebes can have what they want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there was a period, arguably still in effect, where anything AWCY?-related would be more likely to garner downvotes because people were sick of the group. I personally liked this in its original form, but then my tastes in this regard don't always match with other people…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came across this article in it's latest version, read the comments, discovered it was rewritten, went back and viewed the original.

Hate to jump on the bandwagon, but I do prefer the original one. I don't understand how anyone could feel that the AWCY? reference was "tacked on" in the original piece. The original might as well have been the distilled essence of AWCY? on a stick. As it is now, it's just a "plank of wood what makes you go crazy". Boring.

I would have upvoted the original piece, but as it is now, no-vote.

Edit: I think the thing is that the original article was almost too perfect of an AWCY? piece, as it only served to troll and enrage the readers to down-vote it into oblivion. It's as if we the readers were the target of the fictional GOI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should read the older version. It gives a interesting and different perspective on the SCP that makes it a bit more creepy, in my opinion. While the current one does bring it up a bit, I still like the feel on the older one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My personal head canon is now that this miror is the reason the AWCY GOI exists.

Wonder if I should reread the older version everyone keeps mentioning or just be happy with it as it is… Hmm…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this comment inspired me to do a -j where it has 2 anomalous effects
the 2nd one is that its classified as an scp yet it is not a mirror

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It even makes sense that the article would be CHANGED to explain the joke - the Foundation was actually taken in by it at first, and the article still contains the evidence of that. It's similar to what happened with SCP-1048, and is still happening with SCP-1173.

Not to mention people were downvoting the original because it was too subtle, if the comments are any indication.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In becoming more plausible, the article lost most of its interesting points. To me, the Foundation was supposed to be "stupid" in the original, or at least rigid enough it couldn't tell someone was messing with them.

And yes, I know the original was badly received. I was just hoping that, though theres probably very little chance, that more people would appreciate the old article after being given the explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have reread this and I dislike this current version, and thusly changed my vote. I also read through the original and found that I liked it much, much more than this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. Also, as I've said elsewhere (maybe in this thread), getting the Foundation to not figure it out while making it patently obvious to anyone out-of-universe is EXACTLY what I would envision AWCY? doing, given the chance. Especially after Silber's explanation of SCP-1802.

Current version is lame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah - I first downvoted, then upvoted when I realized what it actually is, then downvoted again with the rewrite.
The point is, it was far more interesting when someone was actually able to fuck with the Foundation like that, though I imagine in-universe, that document wasn't very old, and it indeed ended up as this.
Hmm. maybe if the original was as an attachment - you know, explicit previous version, with the stuff getting properly classified afterwards when they figured.
That said, it's at over +50 so meh. I'll just grind my teeth and go elsewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You realize it was being downvoted into oblivion at the time I took over, right?

The version you liked has the Foundation being stupid and gullible. This version has the Foundation shaking that off and exploring the effect scientifically, which is the whole point of the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. So much this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The original article worked on so many levels and was brilliant. The rewrite is just an explanation for the joke. Wish there was a vote to revert.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The original version of the article spelled it like that the whole way through. People hated it.

I wish it was still that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The original version of the article spelled it like that the whole way through. People hated it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly liked this much better in its original form — AWCY? seems like exactly the kind of people to do something that, on the off-chance that they are fictional, would troll the readers in real life. Like with SCP-1802. Or at the very least, it seems something that someone who aligns themselves with the group would do.

Now it's just… a troll mirror. With AWCY? tacked on at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw the miror typo, I wished there was more miror typos along the article. Nonetheless, pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it. It's just a miror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All sighted individuals, regardless of whether they've seen it first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, I hate AWCY?

Not AWCY?-linked articles, understand. Nor the people that write them. Nor the concept. The actual* group. Like Magritte and his fucking pipe, I just want to punch them all in the neck.

Thus, for me, is illustrated the difference between "I hate this SCP" and "This SCP sucks".

+1. Dammit.

*fictional

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aw man, I already upvoted. :(

Still like this, though, especially that bit about it fooling touch. But, does that affect all sighted persons, or only individuals who have looked at the item first?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I now actually understand this. +1.

item.title: Oh man, it makes sense to people who aren't me now!
item.content:encoded:
Well done, Voct.

I had high hopes; you have surpassed them. I'd upvote if I'd not made the page. Proceed directly to go, collect two hundred dollars.

Edit: I do wish you'd left in the persistent "miror" typo, though.

item.title: Re: REWRITE
item.content:encoded:
You know what? I kinda like this now.

+1

item.title: Re: REWRITE
item.content:encoded: Gratuitous approval. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cool now. Downvote reversed.

item.title: REWRITE
item.content:encoded:
With permission from Yorick and Bouncl, I have rewritten this SCP completely.

It still has the exact same concept, but now the Foundation isn't being stupid. The Foundation has figured out what the fuck this thing is doing, and is spelling it out so that whoever else is assigned to it will be able to not get killed.

It is a sign that makes you think it is a mirror, and when you look at your "reflection" (which isn't really a reflection because it's not a mirror), you think you see something wrong with your body, so you feel this overwhelming need to 'correct' yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooooh, I actually like that one. I assumed that it was one of those cliche "It's AWCY because it says 'are we cool yet?' along with a cryptic note" deals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, just a figure of speech, if I understand rightly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: scp-1800

item.title: Re: Staff Post?
item.content:encoded: Funnily enough, reading through all that, I thought he was play acting being completely pretentious because it was "Are We Cool Yet?". A literal rendition of what one of them would be like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, well when you say that you have no idea why anybody could dislike this scp when it's been explained several times- the effect is bland, the idea is cool but the article isn't actually a good story- you might as well just say that everyone downvoting really is too dumb to 'get it.'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, my original vote was a downvote, because of a reflexive, "This is like something I did, except I didn't do it, so I hate it." But it's actually well-done. I have no idea why it's being downvoted.

What's more, I have no idea why it's so subtle. It shouldn't be so subtle! It's a picture of something which very clearly isn't a mirror. And yet everyone here in the comments is actually treating it like a mirror. And in fact, I did that myself when I saw it. Which is… frankly, that's nearly anomalous itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe that the storytelling, the actual presentation of that information, is not equal to the cleverness of the idea; incomprehensibility can only be taken so far…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Description: SCP-1207 is a miror approximately one meter by one half meter in size, in a white wooden frame painted with pink and red hearts. Using the Kinscale method of miror-type object evaluation, Foundation scientists have established it as a minor level psychological threat.

Aside from the apology that it's another mirror by saying it's another mirror. I… hate to say that I like this. It's an extremely well written safe object with more serious implications than are on the surface, and I'm always for SCPs that the Foundation mislabels or otherwise doesn't know everything about. Them knowing everything is just ridiculous.

I have no idea why this is being downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nevermind, didn't see the lower conversation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am a typo.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: for somebody who allegedly writes things you have an extremely poor understanding of literary device

item.title: Re: Staff Post?
item.content:encoded:
And that makes it officially rudeness to an Administrator in an official capacity. Aburr, your membership is rescinded per current rules for 24 hours. You may reapply at that time if you wish to.

On a side note: If Aburr had kept his mouth shut, the other admins would have talked me down to limiting it to a warning. Let me reiterate: please do not respond to official staff posts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But, it does not, in fact, blow dongs. it is a fictional construct, incapable of performing an action, literally. You're still using the word wrong. Literally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Blow a dong" in this instance would be a metaphor, so saying that it happened figuratively would be redundant.

So, right, stick with me, it is the tension between metaphorical language and the word literal, in its capacity both as a descriptor of the actual and as an informal intensifier, which creates the emotive frisson necessary to communicate my feelings on the value of the AWCY connection.

Which is that it blows dongs.

Literally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted because even after reading about and understanding the nature of the object, I still didn't really "get it." It's a miror that isn't a mirror. The AWCY reference feels sort of tacked on as well.

Downvote provisionally removed after rereading and seeing "mirors wing at Site 29" and "Kinscale method of miror-type object evaluation."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still using it wrong. It figuratively blows a dong. Please to not be using words you do not understand, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
rape vending machine

Wait… what?

item.title: Re: Staff Post?
item.content:encoded: Yeah, well, amen to that shit.

item.title: Re: Staff Post?
item.content:encoded: In the immortal words of senior staff: "Don't respond to a staff post." It's all boring and stuff, dude. You wouldn't be interested.
